message:
fix(engine): hash completeness, receipt determinism, and correctness fixes

proof_ingestion:
- Receipt ID derivation now includes all witness hashes (transformation,
  equivalence evidence, rollback token), stage, and timestamp in the
  preimage — was previously only hypothesis_id + timestamp, making
  receipts for different stages of the same hypothesis collide
- New specialization_receipt_preimage() builds the full canonical input
- Signature now signs the same complete preimage as the receipt ID

resource_certificate_consumer:
- New EnforcementReceiptHashPreimage struct for structured, reproducible
  receipt hashing via serde_json canonical serialization
- compute_serialized_content_hash() helper with domain tagging and
  length-prefixed payload for collision resistance
- Policy hash now includes emit_violation_details flag — previously two
  policies differing only in this flag produced identical hashes
- Receipt content hash now covers certificate_id, budget_snapshot,
  decision_sequence, and policy_hash (were previously omitted)
- Tests verify hash sensitivity to policy and receipt variations

native_addon_membrane:
- Bug fix: use local `violations` vec (computed from current evaluation)
  instead of `state.violations` (stale from prior evaluations) when
  determining Degraded verdict — was masking real-time violations

versioned_rewrite_pack:
- Remove dead code: rule_target_matches_pack() function was unused after
  prior refactor introduced rule_target_rule_id_for_pack()

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
